analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-501

POL-501.title: IMPLEMENT: Provider-Agnostic QC Repair Loop
POL-501.body:
## Objective

Implement a provider-agnostic Quality Control repair loop for Polaris so QC providers review PR/local diffs, Polaris normalizes findings, compiles bounded repair packets, dispatches repair workers through the governed Foreman loop, reruns QC after repairs, and stops after a configured maximum number of repair rounds.

## Context

[POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) analyzed the follow-on architecture needed after the existing QC foundation. Current code already defines provider-neutral QC findings and artifacts under `src/qc/`, terminal QC gates in `src/finalize/`, QC run pointers in `src/cluster-state/`, and SOL QC scoring in `src/autoresearch/`. It does not yet support configured provider fallback, provider failure classification, repair-packet compilation, bounded repair rounds, Foreman discovery of QC repair work, or round-level SOL telemetry.

## Goal

Deliver a bounded QC repair lifecycle where external QC providers remain critics, Polaris owns normalization and routing, repair work is executed by normal governed workers, and unresolved findings escalate deterministically to operator review or Medic rather than creating an unbounded loop.

## Scope

* Update QC architecture docs and route-local guidance for provider-agnostic repair loops.
* Extend QC provider config, validation, and CodeRabbit configuration to cover command, args, output format/parser, timeout, fallback, and failure/rate-limit policies.
* Normalize provider success, findings, parse failures, timeouts, rate limits, and all-provider-failed outcomes.
* Compile normalized findings into repair packet manifests with grouping, overlap, severity, attribution, and safety rules.
* Integrate repair packet discovery with Foreman/loop state so repair workers execute via governed dispatch, including bounded post-repair QC reruns.
* Emit SOL-readable telemetry and escalation outcomes for QC rounds, provider attempts, repair packets, max-round stops, and Medic referrals.

## Acceptance Criteria

* QC disabled behavior remains unchanged.
* CodeRabbit remains the first concrete provider, but no runtime path assumes CodeRabbit is always available.
* Provider failures are classified and routed by policy instead of collapsing into generic failed QC results.
* Repair packets are durable cluster artifacts and include allowed scope, source findings, validation expectations, conflict/parallelism metadata, and routing target.
* Foreman discovers and dispatches repair packets as governed repair work, not as inline parent edits.
* QC reruns after repair workers complete and stops after the configured max repair rounds, initially defaulting to 2.
* Unresolved findings after max rounds produce operator review or Medic referral evidence.
* SOL/autoresearch can read QC repair loop telemetry and summarize provider, packet, round, repair, and escalation outcomes.

## Validation

* Add/update focused unit tests for config schema/validator, provider failure classification, normalized result parsing, repair packet compiler grouping/conflict rules, bounded state transitions, and SOL evidence loading.
* Add integration coverage for completed-cluster QC finding -> repair packets -> repair worker dispatch state -> post-repair rerun -> max-round or pass outcome.
* Run the relevant test suites for config, QC, cluster-state, loop/finalize integration, and autoresearch/SOL scoring.
* Verify `polaris.config.json` continues to validate and the default disabled QC path has no behavior change.

## Ordering

1. Document architecture and lifecycle contract.
2. Extend provider config and fallback policy.
3. Normalize provider results and failure classes.
4. Compile findings into repair packets.
5. Orchestrate bounded repair rounds and Foreman discovery.
6. Emit SOL telemetry and escalation outcomes.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement provider-specific network APIs beyond the local CodeRabbit command/config path.
* Do not treat QC providers as normal worker providers.
* Do not silently mutate code from QC provider suggestions.
* Do not allow unbounded repair loops.
* Do not replace Polaris validation, finalize evidence gates, Closeout Librarian, or human PR review.

POL-507.title: Emit SOL telemetry and escalation outcomes for QC repair loops
POL-507.body:
## Objective

Extend SOL/autoresearch evidence and reports so QC repair loops expose provider attempts, repair rounds, packet outcomes, max-round stops, operator review, and Medic referrals.

## Context

`src/autoresearch/sol-evidence-loader.ts` and `src/autoresearch/score.ts` already summarize QC artifacts and count repaired/autofixed/waived/open findings. [POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) requires round-level and provider-attempt-level telemetry so SOL can evaluate whether QC repair loops are effective or noisy.

## Goal

Make QC repair loop outcomes observable in SOL without letting SOL block delivery by itself: SOL should report trends, penalties, provider noise, repair effectiveness, and recommended follow-up actions.

## Scope

* Extend SOL evidence types for QC provider attempts, fallback outcomes, repair round counts, compiled packet counts, repair packet results, rerun outcomes, max-round stops, all-provider-failed, operator review, and Medic referral.
* Update `src/autoresearch/sol-evidence-loader.ts`, `src/autoresearch/score.ts`, `src/autoresearch/sol-scorer.ts`, `src/autoresearch/sol-recommendations.ts`, and reports as needed.
* Consume telemetry emitted by [POL-504](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-504/normalize-qc-provider-results-and-failure-classifications)/[POL-506](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-506/orchestrate-bounded-qc-repair-rounds-and-foreman-discovery) and QC artifacts from [POL-505](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-505/compile-qc-findings-into-bounded-repair-packets).
* Add recurring signals for noisy providers, repeated repair failures, unresolved high-severity findings, and max-round exhaustion.
* Ensure unavailable/future evidence states remain clear when QC is disabled or no repair loop ran.

## Acceptance Criteria

* SOL evidence distinguishes no QC configured, QC ran with no findings, QC repaired findings, QC reached max rounds, all providers failed, operator review required, and Medic referral required.
* SOL scoring weights QC repair outcomes without treating provider findings as ground truth.
* Reports include provider breakdown, round count, packet count, repaired/open findings, unresolved severity, and escalation outcome.
* Existing SOL tests continue to pass for runs without QC artifacts.
* New telemetry fields are backward-compatible with older run artifacts.

## Validation

* Add unit tests for SOL evidence loading, QC summary scoring, recommendation generation, and report rendering with QC repair loop fixtures.
* Include fixtures for disabled QC, provider failure, repair success, max rounds reached, and Medic referral.
* Run autoresearch/SOL test suites plus QC telemetry tests.

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-506](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-506/orchestrate-bounded-qc-repair-rounds-and-foreman-discovery). This is the final child because it consumes provider failure, repair packet, and runtime round telemetry surfaces.

## Non-goals

* Do not change runtime repair-loop behavior in this child except telemetry compatibility fixes.
* Do not let SOL directly block delivery.
* Do not require QC artifacts for non-QC runs.

POL-506.title: Orchestrate bounded QC repair rounds and Foreman discovery
POL-506.body:
## Objective

Integrate QC repair packet manifests with the governed loop so Foreman discovers repair packets, dispatches repair workers, reruns QC after repairs, and stops after bounded repair rounds.

## Context

Current QC orchestration is a terminal gate in `src/finalize/index.ts`; it can pass, warn, or block. [POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) requires the repair lifecycle to be represented in runtime state before delivery: compile packets, dispatch repair workers, collect results, rerun QC, and escalate after max rounds.

## Goal

Make QC repair rounds a first-class governed runtime flow while preserving the parent/orchestrator boundary: Foreman coordinates and dispatches; workers implement repairs; QC providers remain external critics.

## Scope

* Extend cluster/run state for QC repair loop metadata: current round, max rounds, source QC run ids, repair packet manifest path, pending/completed repair packet ids, rerun status, and terminal outcome.
* Add Foreman/loop discovery of repair packet manifests from [POL-505](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-505/compile-qc-findings-into-bounded-repair-packets) and convert eligible packets into open repair work without hand-crafted state corruption.
* Ensure repair workers use `worker_role: repair`, normal sealed packet/result contracts, scoped allowed/prohibited paths, and existing dispatch boundary enforcement.
* Run post-repair QC reruns and consume updated normalized results.
* Stop after configured max repair rounds, initially defaulting to 2.
* Preserve finalize behavior when QC is disabled or no repair packets are generated.
* Integrate Medic referral for failed repair worker results, state repair needs, or unresolved high-risk failures according to existing Medic dispatch boundaries.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Foreman can discover compiled QC repair packets and dispatch them as governed repair workers.
* Parent/orchestrator never implements repair code inline.
* Repair packets with non-overlapping safe scopes can be scheduled in parallel within existing worker-pool rules; overlapping/conflicting packets are serialized or blocked.
* QC reruns after repair workers complete and updates finding status to repaired/open/follow-up/operator-review as evidence allows.
* The loop exits deterministically on pass, no repairable packets, max rounds reached, all providers failed, operator review, or Medic referral.
* Existing finalize QC gates do not bypass repair-loop state when repair routing is enabled.

## Validation

* Add integration tests for completed-cluster QC findings -> repair packet manifest -> repair worker dispatch state -> repair completion -> QC rerun -> pass.
* Add tests for max rounds reached, no repairable packets, all providers failed, failed repair worker Medic referral, and overlapping packet serialization.
* Run loop, finalize, QC, and cluster-state test suites impacted by the runtime integration.

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-505](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-505/compile-qc-findings-into-bounded-repair-packets). Must complete before SOL telemetry can report full round and repair outcomes.

## Non-goals

* Do not add new QC providers.
* Do not make QC mandatory.
* Do not bypass existing dispatch boundary or worker result contracts.

POL-505.title: Compile QC findings into bounded repair packets
POL-505.body:
## Objective

Add a QC repair packet compiler that turns normalized actionable findings into durable governed repair packet manifests with safe grouping, scope, ordering, and parallelism metadata.

## Context

[POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) requires Polaris to compile provider findings into repair work rather than letting QC providers mutate code or leaving finalize to block at the end. `src/qc/types.ts` already has findings and routing decisions, while `src/loop/worker-packet.ts` already has a `repair` worker role. The missing piece is a deterministic compiler between normalized findings and governed repair dispatch.

## Goal

Produce cluster-scoped repair packet manifests that Foreman can discover and route to normal repair workers, with enough metadata to avoid unsafe parallel repairs and to escalate broad or high-risk findings.

## Scope

* Add a QC repair packet compiler under `src/qc/` with focused types, schema validation, and artifact persistence.
* Write manifests under `.polaris/clusters/<cluster-id>/qc/repair-rounds/<round>/repair-packets.json`.
* Include packet id, round, source QC run ids, finding ids, severity floor, root-cause hint, allowed/prohibited scope, validation commands, routing target, parallel group, conflicts, Medic flag, and status.
* Implement grouping by same file, overlapping/adjacent ranges, root cause/category, subsystem/route, severity, attribution confidence, and safety risk.
* Detect overlap/conflicts so repair packets that touch the same file or shared governance/config scope are not marked parallel-safe.
* Route broad, low-confidence, pre-existing, critical/high, or unsafe findings to follow-up/operator/Medic outcomes as policy requires.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Compiler consumes normalized successful QC findings and ignores provider failure artifacts except where policy requires operator review.
* Compiler emits deterministic manifests for the same inputs.
* Packet grouping is conservative and never merges unrelated security/auth/data-loss/migration/governance findings with normal repairs.
* Parallel groups are assigned only when scopes are disjoint and dependency-safe.
* Manifests are persisted as durable cluster artifacts and validate against a schema.

## Validation

* Add unit tests for grouping, conflict detection, severity thresholds, attribution confidence, follow-up/operator routing, and deterministic artifact output.
* Add fixtures with same-file findings, cross-file subsystem findings, overlapping scopes, high-risk categories, and low-confidence broad findings.
* Run QC tests and any cluster artifact validation tests.

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-504](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-504/normalize-qc-provider-results-and-failure-classifications). Must complete before Foreman can discover and dispatch repair packets.

## Non-goals

* Do not run repair workers in this child.
* Do not rerun QC in this child.
* Do not alter finalize gates except as needed for compiler artifacts/tests.

POL-504.title: Normalize QC provider results and failure classifications
POL-504.body:
## Objective

Normalize QC provider execution outcomes so Polaris can distinguish valid findings from provider failures, fallback events, unusable output, and all-provider failure.

## Context

`src/qc/runner.ts` currently turns timeouts and parse failures into synthetic failed `QcResult` objects, and `src/qc/providers/coderabbit.ts` parses valid finding payloads. [POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) needs a richer provider-attempt model that can drive fallback policy, repair-loop state, and SOL telemetry.

## Goal

Create a provider-neutral result/failure classification layer that records provider attempts, parses valid findings, classifies non-finding failures, and exposes deterministic policy inputs for downstream repair packet compilation.

## Scope

* Extend QC types/schemas for provider attempt status, failure reason, fallback source, raw output availability, parser result, and all-provider-failed outcome.
* Update `src/qc/runner.ts` to classify timeout, rate-limited, auth failure, command-not-found, nonzero-exit, parse-failed, empty-output, unsupported-mode, and unavailable-provider cases.
* Update CodeRabbit parsing to consume configured output format/parser fields from [POL-503](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-503/extend-qc-provider-configuration-and-fallback-policy).
* Update artifacts so provider attempt evidence is durably linked to normalized `QcResult` artifacts without retaining unsafe raw output by default.
* Add telemetry events for provider attempted, provider failed, fallback attempted, fallback succeeded, and all providers failed.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Valid provider findings still produce normalized `QcFinding[]` compatible with existing SOL QC summary logic.
* Provider failures are not confused with provider findings.
* Policy can tell whether a failure should fallback, block, follow-up, or skip based on config.
* CodeRabbit parser remains compatible with JSON, JSONL, and metrics payloads.
* All-provider-failed emits a deterministic result and telemetry event.

## Validation

* Add/update tests for `src/qc/runner.ts`, provider parsers, QC schemas, and policy decisions.
* Include test fixtures for timeout, parse failure, empty output, nonzero exit, rate limit text, missing command, and valid findings.
* Run QC unit tests and config tests.

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-503](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-503/extend-qc-provider-configuration-and-fallback-policy). Must complete before repair packet compiler can safely consume normalized findings and skip provider failure artifacts.

## Non-goals

* Do not compile repair packets in this child.
* Do not dispatch workers.
* Do not add provider-specific remote API integrations.

POL-503.title: Extend QC provider configuration and fallback policy
POL-503.body:
## Objective

Extend Polaris QC configuration so providers are configured through a provider-agnostic execution and fallback model rather than hardcoded CodeRabbit behavior.

## Context

`src/config/schema.ts` and `src/config/validator.ts` currently support provider name, mode, trigger, capabilities, auto-fix eligibility, and severity mapping. [POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) requires command/args/output parser config, fallback policies, timeout/rate-limit behavior, and an explicit CodeRabbit config path while preserving disabled-QC behavior.

## Goal

Make QC provider configuration expressive enough for CodeRabbit-first operation and future providers without changing the normalized finding schema or treating QC providers as worker providers.

## Scope

* Extend `QcProviderConfig` and `schema.json` with command, args, output format, parser, timeout, primary/fallback, failure policy, rate-limit policy, retry policy if needed, and artifact policy if needed.
* Update `src/config/defaults.ts`, loader tests, schema tests, and validator tests.
* Update `polaris.config.json` CodeRabbit entry to use the new explicit provider execution shape.
* Update `src/qc/provider.ts`, `src/qc/registry.ts`, and runner/provider construction as needed so provider commands can come from config.
* Preserve existing simple config compatibility where possible or document migration clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Existing configs without `qc` or with `qc.enabled: false` behave exactly as before.
* `polaris.config.json` validates with an explicit CodeRabbit command/parser configuration.
* Provider config can express primary/fallback provider ordering and policy for timeout, rate-limit, parse failure, and all-provider failure.
* QC provider config remains separate from `execution.providers` and worker router provider policy.
* Invalid provider fields produce clear validation errors.

## Validation

* Add/update unit tests in `src/config/validator.test.ts`, `src/config/loader.test.ts`, and schema-related tests.
* Run config validation tests.
* Run any QC provider registry/runner tests impacted by config construction.
* Verify `polaris.config.json` validates.

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-502](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-502/document-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop-architecture). Must complete before provider failure normalization and repair packet compilation depend on the new provider config fields.

## Non-goals

* Do not add a second concrete provider in this child.
* Do not implement repair packet compilation.
* Do not route repair workers from QC findings yet.

POL-502.title: Document provider-agnostic QC repair loop architecture
POL-502.body:
## Objective

Update Polaris QC documentation and route-local guidance so the provider-agnostic repair loop has an authoritative implementation contract before runtime code changes land.

## Context

Existing docs define QC as an external critic and describe current artifact/routing boundaries, but [POL-500](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-500/analyze-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop) adds a new governed repair-loop lifecycle: provider attempts, normalized failures, repair packet compilation, Foreman discovery, bounded reruns, and SOL telemetry.

## Goal

Give implementers a precise architecture surface for provider config, normalized provider failures, repair packet manifests, bounded state transitions, parallel safety rules, max-round stops, operator review, and Medic referrals.

## Scope

* Update `smartdocs/specs/active/quality-control-architecture.md` with the provider-agnostic repair loop contract.
* Update `smartdocs/architecture/quality-control-lifecycle.md` with bounded repair-round lifecycle placement.
* Add or update route-local guidance for `src/qc/`, `src/loop/`, `src/finalize/`, `src/cluster-state/`, and `src/autoresearch/` if missing or stale.
* Document repair packet manifest fields, grouping rules, conflict/parallelism rules, max-round behavior, and escalation outcomes.
* Reference `smartdocs/raw/analysis/pol-500-provider-agnostic-qc-repair-loop-analysis.md` as source analysis.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Docs clearly distinguish QC providers from worker providers.
* Docs state that QC providers never mutate code directly and repair workers execute through governed dispatch.
* Docs define initial max repair rounds as 2 unless overridden by config.
* Docs specify how repair packets are grouped and when they may run in parallel.
* Docs define terminal outcomes: pass, follow-up/log, operator review, max rounds reached, Medic referral, all providers failed.

## Validation

* Review the updated docs for consistency with existing [POL-461](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-461/analyze-polaris-quality-control-architecture)/[POL-471](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-471/document-qc-architecture-and-lifecycle-boundaries) QC architecture.
* Verify all referenced paths exist or are intentionally called out as new implementation surfaces.
* Run markdown/link checks if available in the repo.

## Ordering

First child. Must land before config/runtime implementation children so later code follows the documented contract.

## Non-goals

* Do not implement runtime code in this child.
* Do not promote unrelated SmartDocs.
* Do not change QC behavior.

POL-471.title: Document QC architecture and lifecycle boundaries
